title: cyperus rotundus var. subcapitatus
intro:
Cyperus rotundus var. subcapitatus, also known as the yellow nutsedge, is a perennial plant belonging to the sedge family, characterized by its yellowish flowers and rhizomatous growth habit.

It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anthelmintic, antispasmodic, and expectorant effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as a natural diuretic. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as an herbal remedy through infusion or decoction, often used to treat conditions like fever, cough, and digestive problems.
